question: Will China’s 2026 annual GDP growth (Y/Y) be below 1.0%?
description:
This market will resolve according to China's Y/Y Growth Rate of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the full year of 2026, as reported in the "Preliminary Accounting Results of GDP" release for the fourth quarter and full year of 2026, scheduled for some time in January, 2027.

The relevant figure may be found in the table titled “Preliminary Accounting Results of GDP for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year of 2026” under “Growth Rate Y/Y (%)” in the row “GDP” and the column “Year 2026”. The annual GDP Y/Y growth rate will still be considered if China’s GDP reporting format changes.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/

If no figure for the full year 2026 Y/Y GDP growth rate is reported, this market will resolve according to the Y/Y growth rate for Q4 2026. If no data for the specified year and quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 4.0–5.0% annual GDP growth for China in 2026 at 89.5%, reflecting economist projections from institutions like the IMF and World Bank that anticipate a structural slowdown to around 4.3–4.8% due to demographics, high debt levels, and a protracted property sector slump. Recent Q3 2024 GDP expansion of 4.6% year-over-year exceeded modest expectations, bolstered by September's central bank rate cuts and mortgage relief, yet full-year growth is tracking below the official 5% target amid weak domestic consumption and deflationary pressures. Ongoing U.S. trade tensions and anticipated fiscal measures from the late 2024 Central Economic Work Conference further anchor probabilities in this moderate range, with upside risks from intensified stimulus weighed against persistent headwinds.

This market will resolve according to China's Y/Y Growth Rate of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) for the full year of 2026, as reported in the "Preliminary Accounting Results of GDP" release for the fourth quarter and full year of 2026, scheduled for some time in January, 2027.

The relevant figure may be found in the table titled “Preliminary Accounting Results of GDP for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year of 2026” under “Growth Rate Y/Y (%)” in the row “GDP” and the column “Year 2026”. The annual GDP Y/Y growth rate will still be considered if China’s GDP reporting format changes.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/

If no figure for the full year 2026 Y/Y GDP growth rate is reported, this market will resolve according to the Y/Y growth rate for Q4 2026. If no data for the specified year and quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 4.0–5.0% annual GDP growth for China in 2026 at 89.5%, reflecting economist projections from institutions like the IMF and World Bank that anticipate a structural slowdown to around 4.3–4.8% due to demographics, high debt levels, and a protracted property sector slump. Recent Q3 2024 GDP expansion of 4.6% year-over-year exceeded modest expectations, bolstered by September's central bank rate cuts and mortgage relief, yet full-year growth is tracking below the official 5% target amid weak domestic consumption and deflationary pressures. Ongoing U.S. trade tensions and anticipated fiscal measures from the late 2024 Central Economic Work Conference further anchor probabilities in this moderate range, with upside risks from intensified stimulus weighed against persistent headwinds.

question: Will Ulf Kristersson be the next Prime Minister of Sweden?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Sweden on September 13, 2026. 

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed and assumes office as Prime Minister of Sweden following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must officially assume office. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister assumes office by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Sweden; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Sweden
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Sweden on September 13, 2026. 

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed and assumes office as Prime Minister of Sweden following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must officially assume office. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister assumes office by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Sweden; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Magdalena Andersson as Sweden's next prime minister, reflecting her Social Democrats' consistent double-digit lead in national polls over Ulf Kristersson's Moderate-led bloc. Recent surveys, including SVT/Sifo's October poll showing Social Democrats at 35-36% versus Moderates at 19%, bolster the red-green alliance's edge ahead of the 2026 election, implying over 50% combined support. Kristersson's minority Tidö government, weakened since the Liberals exited the cabinet in June 2024 while maintaining partial parliamentary backing alongside Sweden Democrats, remains vulnerable to no-confidence motions but has survived recent challenges. Lower odds for Jimmie Åkesson, Ebba Busch, and others underscore bloc dynamics favoring established leaders, with no snap election signaled.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Slovenia
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket slightly favors incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob at 51% implied probability to remain Slovenia's next leader, amid a tight race with opposition leader Janez Janša at 42.5%, reflecting uncertainty following the collapse of Golob's center-left coalition in late September 2024 over budget disputes and the withdrawal of Social Democrats' support. Recent polls show Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party gaining ground at around 30-35% nationally versus Golob's Freedom Movement at 20-25%, yet traders price in Golob's incumbency edge and potential to assemble a new parliamentary majority by the October 21 deadline set by President Nataša Pirc Musar. A successful cabinet vote could widen Golob's lead, while failure would trigger snap elections likely favoring Janša; upcoming parliamentary sessions on government formation represent key catalysts.

question: Will Aubry Bracco win Survivor Season 50?

If Survivor season 50 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Survivor season 50 has otherwise not concluded by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "Other". eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment crowns Aubry Bracco as the overwhelming Survivor 50 favorite at 87.5% implied probability, propelled by her masterful strategic gameplay in returnee showdowns like Game Changers and Edge of Extinction, aligning perfectly with the season's fan-voted all-legends cast. Recent catalysts include the October 2024 closure of the historic fan ballot selecting 24 players—confirming powerhouses like Cirie Fields (7%) and Ozzy Lusth (0.3%)—and Jeff Probst's interviews emphasizing social adaptability over raw athleticism, boosting Aubry's edge. With filming slated for early 2025, pre-season buzz and blindside potential keep markets volatile ahead of the April premiere.

question: Will Eurozone annual GDP growth in 2026 be less than 0%?
description:
This market will resolve according to the estimation of Euro Area (Eurozone) annual GDP growth for the full year of 2026 (% change), based on seasonally and calendar adjusted quarterly data, as reported in the "GDP and employment flash estimates for the fourth quarter of 2026" flash release for Q4 of 2026, scheduled to be released in January 2027.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/euro-indicators

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the Euro Area GDP growth rate for the full year of 2026 is included in this release, this market will resolve according to the Euro Area GDP growth rate for Q4 2026, as compared to the same quarter in the previous year. If no data is released for either the full year or fourth quarter of 2026 by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter, as compared to the same quarter in the previous year.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced flash GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release of the specified report will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Eurozone 2026 annual GDP growth tilts toward modest expansion, with the 1.0-2.0% bin leading at 35.5% implied probability, aligning closely with consensus forecasts from the ECB (1.6%) and IMF (1.5%) that project a rebound from 2024's subdued 0.8% pace. Competitive dynamics pit this against near-term stagnation odds (0-1.0% at 25.9%) amid Germany's industrial slump and fiscal austerity, while tail risks—ECB rate path, energy volatility, and potential U.S. tariffs—elevate extremes like 7.0%+ (24.1%) on stimulus bets and <0% (19.9%) recession fears. Key differentiators include December ECB meeting signals and Q4 GDP releases, which could shift market-implied odds as traders price structural headwinds versus monetary easing.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/euro-indicators

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If no data for the Euro Area GDP growth rate for the full year of 2026 is included in this release, this market will resolve according to the Euro Area GDP growth rate for Q4 2026, as compared to the same quarter in the previous year. If no data is released for either the full year or fourth quarter of 2026 by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter, as compared to the same quarter in the previous year.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced flash GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release of the specified report will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Eurozone 2026 annual GDP growth tilts toward modest expansion, with the 1.0-2.0% bin leading at 35.5% implied probability, aligning closely with consensus forecasts from the ECB (1.6%) and IMF (1.5%) that project a rebound from 2024's subdued 0.8% pace. Competitive dynamics pit this against near-term stagnation odds (0-1.0% at 25.9%) amid Germany's industrial slump and fiscal austerity, while tail risks—ECB rate path, energy volatility, and potential U.S. tariffs—elevate extremes like 7.0%+ (24.1%) on stimulus bets and <0% (19.9%) recession fears. Key differentiators include December ECB meeting signals and Q4 GDP releases, which could shift market-implied odds as traders price structural headwinds versus monetary easing.

question: Will Arsenal win a trophy this season?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Arsenal is officially crowned the winner of at least one of the following competitions: the 2025–26 English Premier League, UEFA Champions League, Carabao Cup or FA Cup. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for Arsenal to win any of the four listed competitions (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated from winning, or eliminated from, all four competitions), the market will immediately resolve to "No".

If one or more of the listed competitions is canceled or is otherwise not completed by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the results of the listed competitions that have been completed at that time. If Arsenal has not won any listed competition by that time, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the organizers of the listed competitions; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Arsenal win a trophy this season?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Arsenal is officially crowned the winner of at least one of the following competitions: the 2025–26 English Premier League, UEFA Champions League, Carabao Cup or FA Cup. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for Arsenal to win any of the four listed competitions (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated from winning, or eliminated from, all four competitions), the market will immediately resolve to "No".

If one or more of the listed competitions is canceled or is otherwise not completed by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the results of the listed competitions that have been completed at that time. If Arsenal has not won any listed competition by that time, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the organizers of the listed competitions; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's surge to the top of the Premier League table, just ahead of rivals on goal difference after a nine-game unbeaten run capped by a 3-0 win over Ipswich, anchors the 93.7% trader consensus for a trophy this season. Their flawless progression through the Carabao Cup to the semifinals against Newcastle, alongside a strong Champions League league phase finish (second place, facing PSV in playoffs) and an upcoming FA Cup third-round bye, multiplies pathways to silverware. Minimal injuries—Odegaard back training, Saka and Rice fully fit—bolster squad depth, while a favorable fixture list post-holiday eases fatigue risks compared to Liverpool's busier schedule. Historical drought aside, current momentum reflects crowd wisdom on Arsenal's defensive solidity (league-best 11 clean sheets) and clinical attack.

question: Will the 2026 Midterm Elections happen as scheduled?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the 2026 US Midterm Elections happen on November 3, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will the 2026 Midterm Elections happen as scheduled?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the 2026 US Midterm Elections happen on November 3, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The U.S. Constitution's Article I, Section 4 firmly sets midterm elections for the first Tuesday after the first Monday in November—November 3, 2026—driving trader consensus to 89% yes on Polymarket for them proceeding as scheduled. No official actions, such as executive orders, congressional bills, or Supreme Court cases, indicate postponement, with unbroken historical precedent across 34 prior cycles. Recent news, including the Federal Election Commission's routine planning updates and the orderly 2024 presidential transition, bolsters this stability without disruptions. Traders discount slim tail risks like national emergencies, prioritizing legal rigidity and institutional continuity in their real-money assessments.

question: Will any of the Big Six EPL clubs miss European football?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if at least one of the following clubs — Arsenal, Chelsea, Liverpool, Manchester City, Manchester United, or Tottenham Hotspur — fails to qualify for any European (UEFA) football competition (UEFA Champions League, UEFA Europa League, or UEFA Conference League) for the 2026-2027 European season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A club will be considered to have qualified for a UEFA competition if it earns entry into any stage of that competition for the 2026–27 season, including any qualifying round or play-off round. Qualification includes qualifying for any of the 2026-2027 UEFA competitions by winning a 2025-2026 UEFA competition.

If European qualification is not officially finalized by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the official determinations available at that time. If at least one of the clubs has not officially qualified for one of the three competitions by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if at least one of the following clubs — Arsenal, Chelsea, Liverpool, Manchester City, Manchester United, or Tottenham Hotspur — fails to qualify for any European (UEFA) football competition (UEFA Champions League, UEFA Europa League, or UEFA Conference League) for the 2026-2027 European season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A club will be considered to have qualified for a UEFA competition if it earns entry into any stage of that competition for the 2026–27 season, including any qualifying round or play-off round. Qualification includes qualifying for any of the 2026-2027 UEFA competitions by winning a 2025-2026 UEFA competition.

If European qualification is not officially finalized by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the official determinations available at that time. If at least one of the clubs has not officially qualified for one of the three competitions by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United's slide to eighth in the Premier League table with 53 points from 35 games anchors the 98.4% implied probability that at least one Big Six club—Arsenal, Chelsea, Liverpool, Manchester City, Manchester United, or Tottenham—will miss European football next season. Trailing Newcastle by four points and facing tough remaining fixtures against Arsenal, Newcastle, and Brighton, United's recent four-match winless streak, key injuries, and reliance on beating Manchester City in the FA Cup final for a Europa League lifeline fuel trader consensus. Chelsea and Tottenham hold firm sixth and fifth with 60 points apiece, while the top three are locked in Champions League spots. A United surge to seventh or an FA Cup upset remains a slim upset possibility, but current form suggests near-certainty of their continental absence.

question: Will any Premier League club reach 90 points?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any English Premier League (EPL) reaches at least 90 points in the 2025-26 Premier League season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025-2026 Premier League season is cancelled or otherwise not completed by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no Premier League club has reached 90 points, this market will resolve to "No" at that time.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League (https://www.premierleague.com/en/tables).

events.title: Will any Premier League club reach 90 points?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any English Premier League (EPL) reaches at least 90 points in the 2025-26 Premier League season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025-2026 Premier League season is cancelled or otherwise not completed by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no Premier League club has reached 90 points, this market will resolve to "No" at that time.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League (https://www.premierleague.com/en/tables).

eventMetadata.context_description: With only three matchdays remaining in the 2023-24 Premier League season, no club can mathematically reach 90 points, driving the 98.3% implied probability on "No." Arsenal leads with 80 points from 35 games (max 89), Manchester City has 78 (max 87), and Liverpool sits at 74 (max 83), per official standings, while all pursuers trail further. Recent stumbles—like Arsenal's 0-0 draw at Manchester City and Liverpool's 3-4 loss to Fulham—cemented this ceiling, erasing faint hopes after tighter races earlier. Barring unprecedented official corrections to results or standings (highly improbable), trader consensus reflects ironclad arithmetic over form or fixtures.

question: Will Belgium advance through the first Eurovision Semi-Final?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" for the listed countries whose candidates for Eurovision 2026 advance according to the results of the Eurovision First Semi-Final, scheduled for May 12, 2026.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to advance from the Eurovision 2026 First Semi-Final based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If this competition is not held or no contestants advance by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of the First Semi-Final for Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Eurovision 2026 First Semi-Final qualifiers hinges on early speculation, with implied probabilities favoring perennial powerhouses like Sweden (often 70%+ odds) and Ukraine due to historical televote dominance and staging prowess, while newcomers face longshots amid uncertain draws. No national selections have launched yet—most begin late 2025—leaving odds tethered to past qualification rates (e.g., Nordic bloc reliability) and geopolitical participation rumors. The May 2025 Basel contest will crown the 2026 host, reshaping auto-qualifiers (Big 5 + host bypass semis). Watch for January 2026 artist announcements and the March draw assigning countries to semis, key catalysts that could swing markets 20-30 points.

The May 2025 Basel contest will crown the 2026 host, reshaping auto-qualifiers (Big 5 + host bypass semis). Watch for January 2026 artist announcements and the March draw assigning countries to semis, key catalysts that could swing markets 20-30 points.

question: Will Serbia advance through the first Eurovision Semi-Final?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" for the listed countries whose candidates for Eurovision 2026 advance according to the results of the Eurovision First Semi-Final, scheduled for May 12, 2026.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to advance from the Eurovision 2026 First Semi-Final based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If this competition is not held or no contestants advance by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of the First Semi-Final for Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Eurovision 2026's Second Semi-Final qualifiers hinges on speculative national selection outcomes, as the host city and semi-final draw remain unannounced post-2025's Basel contest. Implied probabilities favor perennial powerhouses like Sweden (post-Melodifestivalen strength), Ukraine (diplomatic voting edge), and Italy (RAI's pop appeal), reflecting historical Big 5 auto-qualifiers and pot allocations from past EBU lotteries. Recent buzz from early 2025 national final previews boosts Nordic and Balkan acts, but volatility looms with artist reveals starting Q4 2025. Key watch: EBU's host bid shortlist by summer 2025, which will trigger the semi draw and reshape market odds amid shifting televote and jury dynamics.
